data: I.Sicily with the permission of the Assessorato Regionale dei Beni Culturali e dell’Identità Siciliana - Dipartimento dei Beni Culturali e dell’Identità Siciliana
Partially reopened following extensive restoration work. Comprehensive collection of archaeological material, predominantly from the West of the island, with an important Etruscan collection.
Centre of Palermo, Piazza Olivella 24, 90100 - Palermo
Siciliae obiacentium insular et Bruttiorum antiquae tabulae cum animadversionibus Georgii Gualtheri

Inscriptiones Bruttiorum Lucaniae Campaniae Siciliae Sardiniae Latinae. Pars posterior. Inscriptiones Siciliae et Sardiniae

T. Mommsen, Inscriptiones Bruttiorum Lucaniae Campaniae Siciliae Sardiniae Latinae. Pars posterior. Inscriptiones Siciliae et Sardiniae, Corpus Inscriptionum Latinarum, consilio et auctoritate Academiae Litterarum Regiae Borussicae editum, 10.2 (Berlin: G. Reimer, 1883)

La Sicilia da Sesto Pompeo a Diocleziano

IULIAEAUGUSTAEIMPERATORISCAESARISLUCISEPTIMISEVERIPERTINACISAUGUSTIPIIPARTHICIARABICIETPARTHICIADIABENICIPONTIFICISMAXIMITRIBUNICIAPOTESTATEIIIIMPERATORISVCONSULISIIPATRISPATRIAERESPUBLICAPANHORMITANORUM

Facsimile Images

Apparatus criticus

  • Text of ILMusPalermo

English translation

Tuuli Ahlholm

To Julia Augusta, (wife) of Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pertinax Augustus Pius, victor in Parthia, Arabia and Parthia Adiabene, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the third time, imperator for the fifth, consul for the second, Father of Fatherland. (Erected by) the citizens of Panhormus.

commentary

Iuliae Augustae Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Septimi Severi Pertinacis Augusti Pii Parthici Arabici et Parthici Adiabenici pontificis maximi tribunicia potestate III imperatoris V consulis II patris patriae res publica Panhormitanorum
Text of ILMusPalermo

To Julia Augusta, (wife) of Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pertinax Augustus Pius, victor in Parthia, Arabia and Parthia Adiabene, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the third time, imperator for the fifth, consul for the second, Father of Fatherland. (Erected by) the citizens of Panhormus.
